title: Invigorating Mexican Hominy Pozole
description: A traditional Mexican dish featuring hominy and pork, infused with the flavors of celery salt and a hint of Mediterranean friendliness, all combined in a delicious and energizing pozole.
instructions: In a large pot, heat 2 tablespoons of oil over medium heat.
Add the chopped onion and cook until it is translucent, about 5 minutes.
Add the minced garlic and cook for an additional minute, stirring constantly.
Add the pork shoulder chunks and cook until they are browned on all sides, about 5 minutes.
Add the hominy, chicken broth, celery salt, and dried oregano to the pot, stirring to combine.
Bring the mixture to a boil, then reduce the heat to low and simmer for 20 minutes.
Use two forks to shred the pork into bite-sized pieces.
Stir in the lime juice and season the pozole with salt and pepper to taste.
Serve the pozole hot, garnished with chopped fresh cilantro or scallions if desired.
Ensure poultry is cooked to an internal temperature of 74°C / 165°F (juices run clear, no pink remaining) before serving.
Cook pork to an internal temperature of 63°C / 145°F and let it rest for 3 minutes before serving.
